title: SNOMED Validation v2: Validate dotbase codebook codes
description:
Validate all SNOMED codes from dotbase codebook against Snowstorm.

**Source:** /Users/thome/gt/senologie/20260110_dotbase_codebook.xlsx
- Column L ('Code (for choice)'): 2,873 entries
- Column N ('Codesystem (SNOMED)'): 3,093 entries  
- **678 unique SNOMED codes** to validate

**Process:**
1. Extract SNOMED codes from columns L and N (numeric 6-18 digit codes)
2. Query Snowstorm for each code
3. Get: validity, FSN, German designation, synonyms (DE+EN)
4. Flag deprecated/replaced codes

**Output:** JSON with structure:
```json
{
  "DGS_Term": "<from Choice column>",
  "DGS_Code": "<SNOMED code>",
  "status": "valid|deprecated|replaced|not_found",
  "currentCode": "...",
  "germanDesignation": "...",
  "englishFSN": "...",
  "synonyms_de": [],
  "synonyms_en": []
}
```

**Note:** Previous attempt (seno-1p3) only validated 29 codes from FSH files - this task covers the full 678 codes.

description: Patrol 1: No polecats. Refinery running. Deacon alive. Inbox clean.

title: SNOMED Validation: Validate and document all SNOMED mappings
description:
Validate all DGS Senologie SNOMED codes against local Snowstorm server (latest German Edition).

**Background:**
- German terms were mapped to SNOMED by clinician ~2 years ago
- Methodology not clean, terms may have changed
- Some response options may not have had SNOMED codes then but do now

**Source:** Original Excel (Senologie_Datensatz_20250606.xlsx) - 9 sheets, 2748 fields

**Deliverable:** JSON feedback document for clinician review before profiling.

**JSON Structure:**
```json
{
  "validationDate": "2026-01-18",
  "snomedVersion": "<from snowstorm>",
  "mappings": [
    {
      "DGS_Term": "Mammakarzinom",
      "DGS_Code": "254837009",
      "status": "valid|deprecated|replaced|not_found|new_mapping",
      "currentCode": "...",
      "germanDesignation": "...",
      "englishFSN": "...",
      "synonyms_de": [],
      "synonyms_en": [],
      "notes": "..."
    }
  ]
}
```

title: Extract SNOMED codes from Excel
description: Parse original Excel file (Senologie_Datensatz_20250606.xlsx) and extract DGS_Term + DGS_Code pairs. Source: 9 sheets, 2748 data fields.

title: Validate codes against Snowstorm
description: Query local Snowstorm server for each code. Get: validity status, FSN, German designation, all synonyms (DE+EN)

title: Search unmapped terms in Snowstorm
description: For response options without SNOMED codes: search Snowstorm for potential matches. Flag as new_mapping if found.

title: Generate JSON feedback report
description: Compile validation results into JSON document for clinician review. Include status flags, designations, synonyms, and notes for changed codes.

title: SNOMED Mapping Quality Assessment
description:
Assess quality of SNOMED mappings in dotbase codebook. 

FINDINGS FROM INITIAL ANALYSIS:
1. Many codes have NO German translation in SNOMED (e.g., 254837009 Mammakarzinom)
2. Some codes are WRONG MAPPINGS (e.g., 439401001 is 'Diagnosis' not 'B3 Läsion')
3. Some have spelling variations (hyphenation differences)

APPROACH:
- Use lookup_snomed_all_designations MCP tool to get German designations
- Compare DGS_Term against German synonyms
- Flag: missing_german, semantic_mismatch, good_match, spelling_variation

OUTPUT: Quality report with categories for manual review

title: Senologie Diagnose E2E Pipeline
description: End-to-end FHIR workflow: Condition profile → IG Publisher → Aidbox → Questionnaire (population+extraction) → SQLonFHIR + Jupyter analytics

title: Create Senologie Condition Profile
description: Define BIH-PR-Seno-Diagnose profile based on Condition resource. Map logical model fields: Diagnosekode (SNOMED), Grad, Seite (laterality), DatumErstdiagnose, Diagnosesicherung, Metastasierungsstadium. Follow existing profile pattern.

title: Get IG Publisher running
description: Ensure IG builds successfully with FHIR IG Publisher. Fix any SUSHI errors, validate dependencies, generate output.

title: Set up local Aidbox instance
description: Configure local Aidbox FHIR server. Docker compose setup, license, initial configuration for R4.

title: Import strategy: IG or direct POST
description: Determine and implement import approach. Options: (1) Import full IG package to Aidbox, (2) POST individual StructureDefinitions. Document chosen approach and scripts.

title: Create Diagnose Questionnaire with population + extraction
description: Build Questionnaire for Diagnose data entry. Implement: (1) Definition-based extraction to create Condition resources, (2) Population from existing Condition data. Use SDC (Structured Data Capture) extensions.

title: SQLonFHIR ViewDefinitions + SQL export
description: Define ViewDefinitions for Condition/Diagnose data. Configure SQL export from Aidbox. Test queries against flattened views.

title: Jupyter notebook analytics layer
description: Set up Jupyter notebooks connecting to SQL views. Create example analyses for Diagnose data: distributions, trends, cohort queries.
